completeness.rationale: All six expected extracts were captured: manual save trigger (implicit in reproduction steps), CSV import with special characters, large task list (~200 tasks), started after CSV import, encoding error message, and exact environment (macOS 14.2, TaskFlow v2.3.1). The triage also discovered additional useful details: the specific character types (em-dashes, curly quotes from Google Docs), the approximate size threshold (~40-50 works, ~200 doesn't), and that removing imported tasks resolves the issue.

accuracy.rationale: The summary is highly accurate about the symptoms and triggering conditions. The root cause hypothesis about multi-byte character handling in serialization is close to the actual cause (save serializer choking on non-ASCII when payload exceeds 64KB). The hypothesis about buffer size calculations assuming single-byte characters is a reasonable approximation of the actual 64KB payload threshold issue. The one gap is that the triage didn't distinguish manual save vs auto-save — the summary says 'crashes on save' without noting auto-save works fine, though the reporter didn't explicitly volunteer this distinction either.

efficiency.rationale: Only 2 question rounds were needed to go from a vague 'app crashes when I save' to a comprehensive, actionable triage. The first question efficiently gathered crash behavior, context, and environment in one round. The second question precisely targeted the CSV/encoding hypothesis. No wasted questions, no redundancy.

question_quality.rationale: The first question was well-structured, covering three orthogonal dimensions (crash behavior, context, environment) without overwhelming the reporter. The second question demonstrated genuine diagnostic reasoning — connecting the CSV import mention and encoding error to form a hypothesis about non-ASCII characters, then asking a targeted confirmation question plus a control experiment (new list with manual tasks). This led the reporter to volunteer the crucial size-dependency information unprompted.

actionability.rationale: The summary is exceptionally actionable. It includes clear reproduction steps, a specific and plausible root cause hypothesis pointing to exact code areas to investigate (serialization buffer calculations, CSV import sanitization), a concrete recommended fix with three specific checks, a proposed test case with boundary testing, and honest confidence levels. A developer could immediately start investigating the serialization code path.

notable_strengths: Exceptional efficiency — extracted comprehensive information in just 2 rounds from a very vague initial report
Second question demonstrated real diagnostic reasoning by connecting CSV import + encoding error into a testable hypothesis
The control experiment suggestion ('create a brand-new task list with manually-typed tasks') elicited the critical size-dependency finding
Summary includes specific Unicode code points (U+2014, U+201C/U+201D) and concrete investigation steps
Honest confidence levels and well-identified information gaps
notable_weaknesses: Did not distinguish manual save vs auto-save — the summary implies all saves crash, but per ground truth only manual save triggers the bug
Root cause hypothesis focuses on buffer size calculations rather than the actual 64KB payload threshold, though this is a reasonable approximation
most_insightful_question: Did the CSV file you imported contain any non-ASCII characters — things like accented letters (é, ñ, ü), emoji, or other special symbols in task names or descriptions? Also, if you create a brand-new task list with just a couple of manually-typed tasks (no imported ones), does saving work fine?
missed_opportunities: Could have asked whether auto-save (if the app has it) also triggers the crash, which would distinguish save-trigger-specific vs serialization-path issues
Could have asked for crash logs from macOS Console.app or DiagnosticReports (though this was noted as an information gap in the summary)
